scope.summary: Delete the four dead metadata-gate retry tags v0.6.2-v0.6.5 from local and origin, leaving every published tag intact.
out_of_scope: Any tag that was actually published (v0.6.0, v0.6.1, v0.6.6 remain untouched).
Deleting GitHub Release objects: none existed for these four tags, re-verified before deletion.
Any source-code change: this close is a remote-ref cleanup plus its tracker record.

entry_points.name: dead-tag precondition re-verified
entry_points.trace: gh release list showed no GitHub Release object attached to v0.6.2, v0.6.3, v0.6.4 or v0.6.5; the bead records only 0.6.0/0.6.1/0.6.6 were ever published. Operator approval to proceed was recorded in the bead description on 2026-07-21 before any deletion. Operator approval was recorded in the bead description on 2026-07-21 before any deletion (RULE 1 satisfied; executed by the lead, not an agent).

entry_points.name: deletion executed local + origin
entry_points.trace: git tag -d and git push origin :refs/tags/<tag> ran for all four tags; each reported deletion.

entry_points.name: published tags intact after deletion
entry_points.trace: git ls-remote --tags origin filtered to the v0.6.x line returned exactly v0.6.0, v0.6.1, v0.6.6 - the four dead tags absent, every published tag still present.

limitations: Tag deletion is an outward-facing, irreversible remote mutation; it was performed only after explicit in-session operator approval was recorded in the bead.
No GitHub Release object existed for any of the four tags, so nothing published was affected; this close does not claim any release-pipeline run.
Verification is the post-deletion git ls-remote listing, not a CI artifact.
Verification was a targeted git ls-remote check, not a Required-graph proof at this SHA; the close therefore claims not-ready.
